_comment: Notable Milky Way objects for the GalacticResource viewer. Each entry has name, category, ICRS equatorial coordinates (RA/Dec), distance in parsecs, and a short note. Distances from SIMBAD / NASA HEASARC / literature. kind: nebula|cluster|remnant|structure|blackhole|galaxy|starforming

objects.name: Sgr A*
objects.note: Supermassive black hole at galactic center, 4.1e6 solar masses

objects.name: Orion Nebula
objects.note: M42, nearest massive star-forming region, ~24 ly across

objects.name: Eagle Nebula
objects.note: M16, Pillars of Creation region, Sagittarius Arm

objects.name: Crab Nebula
objects.note: M1, remnant of SN 1054 supernova, contains Crab Pulsar

objects.name: Eta Carinae
objects.note: Luminous blue variable binary system, candidate for future supernova

objects.name: Lagoon Nebula
objects.note: M8, giant interstellar cloud in Sagittarius

objects.name: Tarantula Nebula
objects.note: 30 Doradus, in LMC, most active starburst in Local Group

objects.name: Rosette Nebula
objects.note: Open cluster NGC 2244 + surrounding emission nebula

objects.name: Horsehead Nebula
objects.note: Dark nebula in Orion, silhouetted against IC 434

objects.name: Veil Nebula
objects.note: Cygnus Loop supernova remnant, ~8000 yr old

objects.name: Omega Centauri
objects.note: Largest globular cluster in Milky Way, ~10 million stars

objects.name: 47 Tucanae
objects.note: Second-brightest globular cluster, contains many millisecond pulsars

objects.name: M13 (Hercules)
objects.note: Great Hercules Cluster, target of Arecibo message 1974

objects.name: Pleiades (M45)
objects.note: Nearest bright open cluster, ~800 stars, age 115 Myr

objects.name: Hyades
objects.note: Nearest open cluster to Sol, age 625 Myr

objects.name: Double Cluster
objects.note: NGC 869 + NGC 884, both in Perseus Arm

objects.name: Andromeda (M31)
objects.note: Nearest large spiral galaxy, collision with Milky Way in ~4.5 Gyr

objects.name: Triangulum (M33)
objects.note: Third-largest galaxy in Local Group

objects.name: LMC
objects.note: Large Magellanic Cloud, Milky Way satellite

objects.name: SMC
objects.note: Small Magellanic Cloud, Milky Way satellite

objects.name: Proxima Centauri
objects.note: Nearest star to Sol, hosts Proxima b (potentially habitable)

objects.name: Alpha Centauri AB
objects.note: Nearest stellar system, closest Sun-like star (Alpha Cen A)

objects.name: Barnard's Star
objects.note: Second-nearest stellar system, highest proper motion known

objects.name: Sirius
objects.note: Brightest star in sky, binary with white dwarf Sirius B

objects.name: Betelgeuse
objects.note: Red supergiant in Orion, candidate for nearby supernova

objects.name: Rigel
objects.note: Blue supergiant in Orion, ~120000x Sol luminosity

objects.name: Polaris
objects.note: Current north celestial pole star, Cepheid variable

objects.name: Vega
objects.note: A0V star with debris disk, former and future pole star

objects.name: TRAPPIST-1
objects.note: Ultra-cool dwarf with 7 terrestrial planets, 3 in habitable zone

objects.name: Kepler-186
objects.note: Hosts Kepler-186f, first Earth-size planet in HZ of another star

objects.name: HD 209458
objects.note: First exoplanet observed in transit (HD 209458 b, Osiris)
